# Understanding the Constant Function <B01>

## Definition

The function <B01> is a constant function that maps any integer input x to the value 1. Regardless of what integer you provide as input, <B01> will always return 1.

## Code Example

```python
# The function <B01> always returns 1
result1 = <B01>(0)    # Returns 1
result2 = <B01>(10)   # Returns 1
result3 = <B01>(-7)   # Returns 1
```

## Key Property

The defining characteristic of <B01> is its invariance: the output is completely independent of the input value. This makes <B01> a true constant function where the return value never changes regardless of the argument provided.

# Understanding the Constant Function <B45>

## Definition

The function <B45> is a constant function that always returns the value 45, regardless of what input is provided. For any integer x, <B45>(x) = 45.

## Code Example

```python
# The function <B45> always returns 45
result1 = <B45>(0)    # Returns 45

```
